records.ety: [Of Scand. origin; cf. Icel. vöndr, akin to Dan. vaand, Goth. wandus; perhaps originally, a pliant twig, and akin to E. wind to turn.]

defs: A small stick; a rod; a verge.
quotes: With good smart blows of a wand on his back.

defs: A rod used by conjurers, diviners, magicians, etc.
quotes: Though he had both spurs and wand, they seemed rather marks of sovereignty than instruments of punishment.
Picus bore a buckler in his hand; His other waved a long divining wand.
